summary: Tight Shakedown opener, strong Bertha/Deal, solid second‑set jams
blurb: The show opens with a high‑energy Shakedown Street and delivers tight performances of Bertha, Deal and the rare Bertha > Greatest Story segue. The second set rolls through Playing In The Band into Uncle John’s Band, followed by an expansive Rhythm Devils > Space stretch. Hornsby’s piano adds extra sparkle while Jerry’s guitar shines throughout.
review: June 1, 1991 at the Los Angeles Coliseum was a chaotic night in the house but the band cut through the noise with classic Dead fire. The set kicks off with an exuberant Shakedown Street that sets a bright tone for the evening, and Jerry’s sparkling lead work on tracks like Candyman and Bertha keeps the momentum rolling. A highlight of the first set is the rare Bertha > Greatest Story Ever Told segue – a seamless transition that showcases both Phil’s solid bass foundation and the dual‑keyboard textures of Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick. The second half delivers one of the year’s most satisfying jams: Playing In The Band into Uncle John’s Band, then a well‑crafted Rhythm Devils > Space segment before moving into I Need A Miracle, Black Peter and a tight Throwing Stones/Not Fade Away finish. Hornsby’s improvisational piano adds extra color, while Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart lock in the groove throughout. Despite a few rough spots (the first‑set PA hiccups and an uneven Walking Blues), the overall performance is strong, making this show a solid entry for any 1991 Dead collection.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings label the show as 'mixed' while one is clearly positive; reviewers repeatedly praise the opening Shakedown Street, Bertha/Deal and the Bertha > Greatest Story segue, and note strong second‑set jams. Negative remarks are limited to a few weak spots (Walking Blues, occasional mix dropouts). This consistent but modestly enthusiastic feedback warrants a modest positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp mix and great instrument separation; it presents the show’s strong performances while minimizing the venue’s chaotic ambience.

key_highlights: High‑energy Shakedown Street opener and tight Bertha/Deal performance
Rare Bertha > Greatest Story Ever Told segue that flows beautifully
Second‑set jam chain: Playing In The Band → Uncle John’s Band → Rhythm Devils > Space
Bruce Hornsby’s piano improvisation adds extra sparkle throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Sparkling guitar tone and inspired leads on Shakedown Street, Candyman, Bertha; occasional mix drop in first set but overall ripping performance.
band_performance.Phil: Sounds great, especially anchoring the Playing In The Band → Uncle John's Band transition with solid bass work.
band_performance.Bob: Provides reliable rhythm guitar that sits well in the mix, contributing to the tight feel of the set.
band_performance.Bruce Hornsby: Piano work praised for improvisational flair and adds extra color to jams throughout the night.
band_performance.Vince: Liked by many reviewers; his keyboard contributions complement Hornsby's playing nicely.
band_performance.Billy: Solid, space‑filled drumming typical of Bill Kreutzmann; Rhythm Devils > Space segment described as well‑played.
